title: Perfect Pan-Searing
description: Learn the technique of pan-searing for a delicious crust on proteins.

steps.instruction: Select your protein: a steak, chicken breast, or fish fillet. Pat it completely dry with paper towels. This is crucial for a good sear!
Season the protein generously on all sides with salt and pepper.
Place a heavy-bottomed skillet (like cast iron) over high heat. Let it get very hot.
Add a high smoke point oil (like avocado or grapeseed oil) to the hot pan. It should shimmer and almost smoke.
Carefully place the protein in the hot pan. You should hear a strong sizzle. Do not move it for the first few minutes.
Sear for 2-4 minutes per side, depending on thickness and desired doneness. Use tongs to flip.
Optional: Add butter, garlic, and herbs (like thyme or rosemary) to the pan during the last minute of cooking. Tilt the pan and spoon the melted butter over the protein (basting).
Remove protein from pan and let it rest on a cutting board for 5-10 minutes before slicing. This allows juices to redistribute.

safety_notes: High heat and oil require extreme caution. Keep a lid nearby in case of flare-ups.
Use sturdy tongs to handle hot protein.
